{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Production Assistant",
  "description": "Fresh2Go Smoothie &amp; Co. is a fast growing food and beverage brand focused on delivering fresh, tasty, and affordable meals and drinks. We operate multiple outlets and specialize in smoothies, milkshakes, sandwiches, shawarma, parfaits, burgers, and other ready to eat meals made with quality ingredients.

Our company is driven by a strong commitment to hygiene, customer satisfaction, and operational excellence. We create a dynamic and supportive work environment where teamwork, innovation, and personal growth are encouraged. At Fresh2Go, employees are valued as key contributors to our success, and we are passionate about building a brand that customers trust and talents are proud to be part of.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production Assistant

Job Location: Ibadan, Oluyole - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Production Assistant will assist in the preparation, packaging, labeling, and handling of products while ensuring cleanliness, quality standards, and efficient workflow within the production unit.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in food production and packaging activities.
	Prepare raw materials and ingredients for production.
	Ensure products are properly labeled and packaged.
	Maintain cleanliness and hygiene in the production area.
	Follow food safety and operational procedures at all times.
	Support inventory tracking of production materials and supplies.
	Operate basic production equipment safely and efficiently.
	Report equipment faults, shortages, or quality issues to supervisors.
	Assist with loading, storage, and movement of products when necessary.
	Ensure production targets and deadlines are met.


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE / OND qualification.
	Previous experience in food production, factory work, or kitchen operations is an advantage.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
	Good physical stamina and attention to detail.
	Strong teamwork and communication skills.
	Willingness to work flexible shifts when required.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: oluwatomisin668@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Production Assistant Job at Fresh2Go Smoothie & Co.




        
    

        
        Fresh2Go Smoothie & Co. is a fast growing food and beverage brand focused on delivering fresh, tasty, and affordable meals and drinks. We operate multiple outlets and specialize in smoothies, milkshakes, sandwiches, shawarma, parfaits, burgers, and other ready to eat meals made with quality ingredients.

Our company is driven by a strong commitment to hygiene, customer satisfaction, and operational excellence. We create a dynamic and supportive work environment where teamwork, innovation, and personal growth are encouraged. At Fresh2Go, employees are valued as key contributors to our success, and we are passionate about building a brand that customers trust and talents are proud to be part of.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production Assistant

Job Location: Ibadan, Oluyole - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Production Assistant will assist in the preparation, packaging, labeling, and handling of products while ensuring cleanliness, quality standards, and efficient workflow within the production unit.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in food production and packaging activities.
	Prepare raw materials and ingredients for production.
	Ensure products are properly labeled and packaged.
	Maintain cleanliness and hygiene in the production area.
	Follow food safety and operational procedures at all times.
	Support inventory tracking of production materials and supplies.
	Operate basic production equipment safely and efficiently.
	Report equipment faults, shortages, or quality issues to supervisors.
	Assist with loading, storage, and movement of products when necessary.
	Ensure production targets and deadlines are met.


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE / OND qualification.
	Previous experience in food production, factory work, or kitchen operations is an advantage.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
	Good physical stamina and attention to detail.
	Strong teamwork and communication skills.
	Willingness to work flexible shifts when required.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Manager",
  "description": "Fresh2Go Smoothie &amp; Co. is a fast growing food and beverage brand focused on delivering fresh, tasty, and affordable meals and drinks. We operate multiple outlets and specialize in smoothies, milkshakes, sandwiches, shawarma, parfaits, burgers, and other ready to eat meals made with quality ingredients.

Our company is driven by a strong commitment to hygiene, customer satisfaction, and operational excellence. We create a dynamic and supportive work environment where teamwork, innovation, and personal growth are encouraged. At Fresh2Go, employees are valued as key contributors to our success, and we are passionate about building a brand that customers trust and talents are proud to be part of.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager

Job Location: Oluyole, Ibadan - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Inventory Management Officer will be responsible for monitoring stock levels, tracking inventory movement, minimizing losses, and ensuring timely replenishment of products.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong analytical skills, excellent attention to detail, and experience in inventory operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor daily inventory levels across warehouse and store locations.
	Track incoming and outgoing stock accurately.
	Conduct regular stock counts and reconcile discrepancies.
	Maintain inventory records using inventory management software or spreadsheets.
	Coordinate with procurement and sales teams to ensure product availability.
	Identify slow-moving, expired, or damaged goods and recommend corrective actions.
	Prepare weekly and monthly inventory reports.
	Ensure compliance with inventory control procedures and company policies.
	Assist in forecasting stock requirements based on sales trends.
	Reduce stock losses, wastage, and shrinkage


Requirements


	1&ndash;3 years of experience in inventory management, warehouse operations
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory management systems.
	Strong organizational and problem-solving skills.
	Good numerical and analytical ability.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: oluwatomisin668@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Inventory Manager Job at Fresh2Go Smoothie & Co.




        
    

        
        Fresh2Go Smoothie & Co. is a fast growing food and beverage brand focused on delivering fresh, tasty, and affordable meals and drinks. We operate multiple outlets and specialize in smoothies, milkshakes, sandwiches, shawarma, parfaits, burgers, and other ready to eat meals made with quality ingredients.

Our company is driven by a strong commitment to hygiene, customer satisfaction, and operational excellence. We create a dynamic and supportive work environment where teamwork, innovation, and personal growth are encouraged. At Fresh2Go, employees are valued as key contributors to our success, and we are passionate about building a brand that customers trust and talents are proud to be part of.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager

Job Location: Oluyole, Ibadan - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Inventory Management Officer will be responsible for monitoring stock levels, tracking inventory movement, minimizing losses, and ensuring timely replenishment of products.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong analytical skills, excellent attention to detail, and experience in inventory operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor daily inventory levels across warehouse and store locations.
	Track incoming and outgoing stock accurately.
	Conduct regular stock counts and reconcile discrepancies.
	Maintain inventory records using inventory management software or spreadsheets.
	Coordinate with procurement and sales teams to ensure product availability.
	Identify slow-moving, expired, or damaged goods and recommend corrective actions.
	Prepare weekly and monthly inventory reports.
	Ensure compliance with inventory control procedures and company policies.
	Assist in forecasting stock requirements based on sales trends.
	Reduce stock losses, wastage, and shrinkage


Requirements


	1–3 years of experience in inventory management, warehouse operations
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory management systems.
	Strong organizational and problem-solving skills.
	Good numerical and analytical ability.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

HR Officer Job at Stretch-it Concepts




        
    

        
        Stretch-it Concepts - We are a human resource outsourcing solutions company, bureau of recruitment, performance management and training and development company situated in Lagos, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Officer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: IT

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are hiring an HR Officer for a growing IT & Technology company. You will support the HR department in managing recruitment, employee relations, HR administration, onboarding, performance management, and staff engagement activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Support recruitment, onboarding, and employee documentation
	Maintain employee records and HR databases
	Assist with attendance, leave, and payroll coordination
	Support employee relations and disciplinary processes
	Coordinate staff engagement and training activities
	Assist with performance management and appraisal processes
	Ensure compliance with company policies and HR procedures
	Prepare HR reports and support daily HR operations.


Requirements


	B.Sc / HND in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field
	4-5 years of HR experience
	Good understanding of HR operations and employee relations
	Strong communication and organizational skills
	Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools

Why You Should Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "COISCO Integrated Resources and Petroleum Limited is a proudly indigenous Oil &amp; Gas company operating in Nigeria&rsquo;s downstream sector. Incorporated as a Limited Liability Company in 2006, the company commenced full operations in December 2007 and has since grown into a trusted name within the industry.

At COISCO, integrity, excellence, service, and respect form the core values that drive everything we do. We are committed to delivering quality products and services with excellence, ensuring that our operations consistently meet industry standards and customer expectations. Our business activities cut across haulage and retail sales, with product lines that include Petrol (PMS), Diesel (AGO), Kerosene (DPK), Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), and Lubricants. Our head office is strategically located at KM 10, Onitsha&ndash;Owerri Road, Ogbaku, Imo State, serving as the operational hub for our expanding network.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Ogbaku, Mbaitoli, Imo
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	We are seeking an Accountant who will be responsible for managing the company&rsquo;s day-to-day accounting operations, ensuring accurate financial reporting, maintaining internal controls, monitoring station revenues, reconciling transactions, and supporting management with timely financial insights for decision-making.
	The ideal candidate must be analytical, highly accurate, commercially aware, and experienced in handling accounting functions within a retail, oil &amp; gas, or fast-paced operational environment.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting &amp; Accounting:


	Maintain accurate accounting records for all business transactions.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports across stations and business operations.
	Prepare monthly management accounts, profit and loss reports, and balance sheet schedules.
	Post journal entries and maintain the general ledger.
	Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities.


Cash Management &amp; Reconciliation:


	Monitor daily station sales, deposits, transfers, and remittances.
	Reconcile cash, POS transactions, bank lodgements, and fuel sales records.
	Perform daily and monthly bank reconciliations.
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies in cash balances, station returns, or accounting records.
	Ensure proper documentation of all receipts and payments.


Accounts Payable &amp; Receivable:


	Process vendor payments and maintain supplier ledgers.
	Reconcile supplier statements and follow up on discrepancies.
	Track receivables, outstanding balances, and customer accounts where applicable.
	Maintain payment schedules and ensure timely settlement of obligations.


Inventory &amp; Operational Controls:


	Reconcile fuel stock against sales and tank readings.
	Monitor inventory movement for PMS, AGO, DPK, lubricants, and non-fuel products.
	Support periodic stock counts and physical verification exercises.
	Review variances in stock, fuel discharge, or sales reports and escalate findings.
	Strengthen controls to minimize leakages, fraud, and operational losses.


Payroll, Tax &amp; Compliance:


	Support payroll preparation and related staff financial documentation.
	Ensure timely remittance of statutory deductions including PAYE, pension, VAT, and withholding tax where applicable.
	Maintain proper accounting documentation for audits and regulatory compliance.
	Support internal and external audit exercises.


Analysis &amp; Decision Support:


	Prepare financial analysis on station profitability, margins, costs, and performance trends.
	Track expenses and budget performance across stations.
	Provide management with financial insights to support business planning and operational decisions.
	Recommend cost-saving opportunities and process improvements.


Requirements
Education:


	Minimum of HND or Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of 10&ndash; 15 years of relevant accounting experience, preferably in:
	
		Oil &amp; Gas / Downstream Retail
		Filling station operations
		Retail accounting
		FMCG or multi-location operations
	
	


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting
	Experience with bank reconciliation and cash management
	Strong understanding of inventory accounting and stock reconciliation
	High proficiency in Microsoft Excel
	Familiarity with accounting software/ERP systems
	Excellent analytical and numerical ability
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy
	High level of integrity and confidentiality
	Good reporting and documentation skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines


Preferred Qualities:


	Experience managing multi-location retail accounting
	Ability to work in a fast-paced operational environment
	Strong problem-solving ability
	Commercial awareness and business-minded approach
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@coiscostancosoil.com.ng or coiscorecruitment@gmail.com using &quot;Accountant Application&quot; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-06",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-13",

    "@type": "Organization",

Accountant Job at COISCO Integrated Resources and Petroleum Limited




        
    

        
        COISCO Integrated Resources and Petroleum Limited is a proudly indigenous Oil & Gas company operating in Nigeria’s downstream sector. Incorporated as a Limited Liability Company in 2006, the company commenced full operations in December 2007 and has since grown into a trusted name within the industry.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



At COISCO, integrity, excellence, service, and respect form the core values that drive everything we do. We are committed to delivering quality products and services with excellence, ensuring that our operations consistently meet industry standards and customer expectations. Our business activities cut across haulage and retail sales, with product lines that include Petrol (PMS), Diesel (AGO), Kerosene (DPK), Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), and Lubricants. Our head office is strategically located at KM 10, Onitsha–Owerri Road, Ogbaku, Imo State, serving as the operational hub for our expanding network.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Ogbaku, Mbaitoli, Imo
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	We are seeking an Accountant who will be responsible for managing the company’s day-to-day accounting operations, ensuring accurate financial reporting, maintaining internal controls, monitoring station revenues, reconciling transactions, and supporting management with timely financial insights for decision-making.
	The ideal candidate must be analytical, highly accurate, commercially aware, and experienced in handling accounting functions within a retail, oil & gas, or fast-paced operational environment.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting & Accounting:


	Maintain accurate accounting records for all business transactions.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly financial reports across stations and business operations.
	Prepare monthly management accounts, profit and loss reports, and balance sheet schedules.
	Post journal entries and maintain the general ledger.
	Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities.


Cash Management & Reconciliation:


	Monitor daily station sales, deposits, transfers, and remittances.
	Reconcile cash, POS transactions, bank lodgements, and fuel sales records.
	Perform daily and monthly bank reconciliations.
	Investigate and resolve discrepancies in cash balances, station returns, or accounting records.
	Ensure proper documentation of all receipts and payments.


Accounts Payable & Receivable:


	Process vendor payments and maintain supplier ledgers.
	Reconcile supplier statements and follow up on discrepancies.
	Track receivables, outstanding balances, and customer accounts where applicable.
	Maintain payment schedules and ensure timely settlement of obligations.


Inventory & Operational Controls:


	Reconcile fuel stock against sales and tank readings.
	Monitor inventory movement for PMS, AGO, DPK, lubricants, and non-fuel products.
	Support periodic stock counts and physical verification exercises.
	Review variances in stock, fuel discharge, or sales reports and escalate findings.
	Strengthen controls to minimize leakages, fraud, and operational losses.


Payroll, Tax & Compliance:


	Support payroll preparation and related staff financial documentation.
	Ensure timely remittance of statutory deductions including PAYE, pension, VAT, and withholding tax where applicable.
	Maintain proper accounting documentation for audits and regulatory compliance.
	Support internal and external audit exercises.


Analysis & Decision Support:


	Prepare financial analysis on station profitability, margins, costs, and performance trends.
	Track expenses and budget performance across stations.
	Provide management with financial insights to support business planning and operational decisions.
	Recommend cost-saving opportunities and process improvements.


Requirements
Education:


	Minimum of HND or Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of 10– 15 years of relevant accounting experience, preferably in:
	
		Oil & Gas / Downstream Retail
		Filling station operations
		Retail accounting
		FMCG or multi-location operations
	
	


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting
	Experience with bank reconciliation and cash management
	Strong understanding of inventory accounting and stock reconciliation
	High proficiency in Microsoft Excel
	Familiarity with accounting software/ERP systems
	Excellent analytical and numerical ability
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy
	High level of integrity and confidentiality
	Good reporting and documentation skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines


Preferred Qualities:


	Experience managing multi-location retail accounting
	Ability to work in a fast-paced operational environment
	Strong problem-solving ability
	Commercial awareness and business-minded approach
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] or [email protected] using "Accountant Application" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Matrix Energy Group is a rapidly growing indigenous and integrated Oil Marketing and Trading Company in Nigeria, which was incorporated November 25, 2004. Over the years, Matrix Energy has grown from a modest supply and distributions operation to a fully integrated petroleum products marketing and trading company with clients and suppliers all over the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: LPG Regional Technician

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The LPG Regional Technician is responsible for the safe, efficient, and compliant technical operations of LPG facilities within the northern region.
	The role ensures optimal plant uptime, equipment integrity, adherence to safety standards, and timely resolution of technical faults across LPG storage, dispensing, and safety systems.


Responsibilities


	Oversee daily LPG plant operations to ensure safe, efficient, and uninterrupted performance of equipment and systems.
	Execute preventive and corrective maintenance on LPG infrastructure, including pumps, compressors, dispensers, and storage tanks.
	Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical, and instrumentation faults promptly to reduce downtime and service disruption.
	Enforce strict adherence to HSE policies, LPG safety standards, and all applicable regulatory requirements.
	Conduct routine safety inspections and proactively identify and mitigate operational risks and hazards.
	Maintain accurate technical documentation, including maintenance logs, equipment performance records, and incident reports.
	Prepare and submit timely reports on plant performance, downtime analysis, and maintenance activities.
	Support installation, commissioning, and decommissioning of LPG equipment and related infrastructure.
	Manage spare parts and consumables inventory to ensure continuous and efficient plant operations.
	Provide technical support and on-the-job guidance to station personnel while coordinating issue resolution with relevant stakeholders.

LPG Regional Technician Job at Matrix Energy Group




        
    

        
        Matrix Energy Group is a rapidly growing indigenous and integrated Oil Marketing and Trading Company in Nigeria, which was incorporated November 25, 2004. Over the years, Matrix Energy has grown from a modest supply and distributions operation to a fully integrated petroleum products marketing and trading company with clients and suppliers all over the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: LPG Regional Technician

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The LPG Regional Technician is responsible for the safe, efficient, and compliant technical operations of LPG facilities within the northern region.
	The role ensures optimal plant uptime, equipment integrity, adherence to safety standards, and timely resolution of technical faults across LPG storage, dispensing, and safety systems.


Responsibilities


	Oversee daily LPG plant operations to ensure safe, efficient, and uninterrupted performance of equipment and systems.
	Execute preventive and corrective maintenance on LPG infrastructure, including pumps, compressors, dispensers, and storage tanks.
	Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical, and instrumentation faults promptly to reduce downtime and service disruption.
	Enforce strict adherence to HSE policies, LPG safety standards, and all applicable regulatory requirements.
	Conduct routine safety inspections and proactively identify and mitigate operational risks and hazards.
	Maintain accurate technical documentation, including maintenance logs, equipment performance records, and incident reports.
	Prepare and submit timely reports on plant performance, downtime analysis, and maintenance activities.
	Support installation, commissioning, and decommissioning of LPG equipment and related infrastructure.
	Manage spare parts and consumables inventory to ensure continuous and efficient plant operations.
	Provide technical support and on-the-job guidance to station personnel while coordinating issue resolution with relevant stakeholders.
